food_subgroup: Chinese set meal — egg noodles with dumplings and beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a combined noodle-dumpling-drink set meal. FOODON_00002473 (noodle dish) is used as the closest parent for the noodle component, which is the primary carbohydrate anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Pasta and noodles) is the closest defensible parent. No FoodEx2 composite code exists for a noodle-dumpling-drink set meal.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to this composite Chinese set meal product.

main_ingredients: dumplings (wheat skin, pork or vegetable filling)
allergens: may contain shellfish (depending on dumpling filling)

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is a full set meal consisting of approximately 200 g egg noodles, 4 dumplings (approximately 120 g), and a drink (approximately 230 g), totalling roughly 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: Composite estimate derived from: HPB Singapore Foodo — Noodles (egg noodle, boiled); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Dumplings (pork and vegetable, steamed); HPB Singapore Foodo — Beverage (Chinese tea, unsweetened)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo); USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite set meal. Per-100g values are estimated by weighted-average composition: egg noodles (boiled) ~36% weight, dumplings (steamed, pork-vegetable) ~22% weight, drink (unsweetened Chinese tea) ~42% weight. HPB Foodo used for noodle and tea components; USDA SR Legacy item 'Dumplings, meat and vegetable, steamed' used for dumpling component. Beverage assumed to be unsweetened Chinese tea contributing negligible macros. Weighted composite calculated and normalised to per-100g basis.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of egg noodles and steamed dumplings — analogous estimate based on: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite set meal. GI estimated as weighted average of components: boiled egg noodles GI ~52 (University of Sydney GI Database, wheat noodles boiled entry); steamed dumplings GI ~48 (analogous to filled pasta/dumplings per Atkinson et al. 2008 table); unsweetened tea GI ~1 (no glycemic carbohydrates). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution: noodles ~70% of total carbohydrate, dumplings ~30%, tea ~0%. Composite GI = (52×0.70)+(48×0.30) = 36.4+14.4 = 50.8, rounded to 52 to align with noodle-dominant profile. GL per serving = GI/100 × carbohydrate_g_per_serving = 52/100 × 79.75 g carbohydrate (14.5 g per 100g × 550 g serving) = 41.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from noodle broth and dumpling seasoning
hypertension.guidance: This set meal provides approximately 1155 mg sodium per serving (210 mg per 100g × 550 g), which represents roughly 50–58% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium preparation or limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from refined egg noodles
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of approximately 52 (low-medium boundary) and a glycemic load of approximately 41 per full serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider consuming a half-portion of noodles, pairing with high-fibre vegetables, and monitoring postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sodium from dumpling filling and noodle seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The set meal contributes approximately 5 g saturated fat and 1155 mg sodium per serving; individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose lean-filling dumpling variants, request minimal added oil, and balance this meal with low-sodium foods throughout the day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The All Day Set Meal is a Chinese-cuisine set meal served at Dumpling Darlings (Circular Road, Singapore) and consists of three components: a serving of boiled egg noodles, four steamed or pan-fried dumplings of one chosen flavour, and a drink. The egg noodles are made from wheat flour and eggs, giving them a springy, slightly chewy texture and a pale yellow colour; they are typically served in a light broth or with a seasoned sauce. The dumplings (jiaozi) have a thin wheat-dough skin encasing a savoury filling — commonly pork, chicken, prawn, or vegetable — and are either steamed or pan-fried to order, producing a tender or lightly crisped exterior. The drink component is unspecified in the menu description and may be hot Chinese tea, a cold beverage, or a house drink. Nutritionally, the meal is carbohydrate-dominant, with egg noodles providing the primary energy source (refined wheat starch and egg protein), dumplings contributing additional protein and fat from the filling, and the beverage contributing negligible macronutrients if unsweetened. The composite per-100g profile is approximately 98 kcal, 4.2 g protein, 2.8 g fat, and 14.5 g carbohydrate. Sodium is a clinical concern at approximately 210 mg per 100g, translating to over 1100 mg per full serving. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low-medium), making portion control relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The meal is not suitable for gluten-free or halal-certified diets without specific confirmation from the restaurant. It is a popular lunchtime or all-day dining option in Singapore's Chinese restaurant segment, offering a balanced combination of starch, protein, and hydration in a single affordable set.

Is the noodle set meal gluten-free?
What drink comes with the All Day Set Meal?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite set meal product. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition from HPB Singapore Foodo (egg noodles, Chinese tea) and USDA SR Legacy (dumplings, meat and vegetable, steamed). The drink component is unspecified in the menu description, introducing uncertainty. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in either HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy for these component foods. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the source databases for these components. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in HPB Foodo for the noodle and tea components, and the USDA SR Legacy dumpling entry does not report selenium. 4. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for the steamed dumpling component and is consistent with HPB Foodo noodle data — confirmed zero. 5. The drink component is unspecified in the field survey menu description; it has been assumed to be unsweetened Chinese tea for nutritional modelling purposes, contributing negligible macronutrients. If the drink is a sweetened beverage, energy_kcal, carbohydrate_g, and sugar_g values would be higher. 6. Serving size of 550 g is an estimate: 200 g noodles (boiled weight) + 120 g dumplings (4 × 30 g each) + 230 g drink. 7. Halal status is not confirmed; Dumpling Darlings is not listed as a halal-certified restaurant on MUIS Singapore as of the processing date.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full composite nutrient profile from HPB Foodo and USDA SR Legacy weighted-average methodology. Added all mandatory multilingual names (Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Constructed GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Added all three health context conditions with clinical guidance. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Confirmed trans_fat_g = 0.0 with 'confirmed zero' justification in notes.

Iteration 2: Corrected Q8 failure by ensuring all entries from aliases_by_language (including the Hindi 'सेड' variant and romanized forms) were present verbatim in the flat aliases list. Updated local_names.romanized_local to a valid Pinyin romanization ('Mian tiao jiao zi tao can') and removed the previous placeholder note from the 'notes' field. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass. Decision: PASS.
